title: Finite LDLT Decomposition Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite LDLT bad diagonal-entry QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact LDLT replay computes D[1,1] as 2 while a malformed row claims 3, isolating a compact scalar equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional LDLT rows only when they add distinct diagonal, pivoting, inertia, Hessian, covariance, SDP, or block-factorization solver pressure; keep LDLT existence, pivoting strategy, sparse algorithms, conditioning, and floating-point stability in theorem or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed rational A, L, D, L^T, product, right-hand side, triangular solve, determinant, and leading minors as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false diagonal-entry row through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay computes the conflicting diagonal value.
Expose the pack through the matrix computation learner and consumer query surfaces.
Keep LDLT existence, pivoting strategy, indefinite factorization, rank-deficient cases, sparse algorithms, floating-point conditioning, and stability claims marked Lean or numerical-honesty horizon.
